sourceMovie.title: Enter the Void
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Enter the Void for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Gaspar Noé keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The fantasy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Perfume: The Story of a Murderer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Enter the Void clicked for you, Perfume: The Story of a Murderer (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Enter the Void?
faq.answer: Try Perfume: The Story of a Murderer, Requiem for a Dream, Midsommar—each shares DNA with Enter the Void while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Enter the Void?
faq.answer: Enter the Void (2010) is best described as Fantasy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Enter the Void worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
